title: Battery Percentage Display After Connection — Poly Voyager Free 60+ UC
steps: Launch Poly Studio Mobile app.
Navigate to: Home → Connect Device → Poly Voyager Free 60+ UC.
Complete Bluetooth pairing process.
Wait for status to display 'Connected' on Home screen.
Observe battery percentage values for Left Earbud, Right Earbud, and Charging Case on Home screen.
Record all three values.
expected: Expected: Left Earbud, Right Earbud, and Charging Case each display a numeric battery percentage (0-100%). Actual (If Bug): One or more battery values show 0% despite 'Connected' status. Regression boundary: No value should remain at 0% for more than 5 seconds after 'Connected' status appears.

title: Battery Data Freshness — Extended Wait After Connection
steps: Launch Poly Studio Mobile app.
Connect to Poly Voyager Free 60+ UC. Wait for 'Connected' status.
Immediately record battery % for Left, Right, and Charging Case.
Keep app open and device connected. Wait 30 seconds.
Re-read battery % for all three.
Wait an additional 60 seconds. Re-read battery % again.
expected: Expected: Battery percentages are populated (non-zero unless truly depleted) at first read and remain populated across all three reads. Actual (If Bug): Battery shows 0% at first read and stays at 0% after 30s and 90s, indicating stale/never-populated battery data. Regression boundary: No battery value should remain at 0% beyond the initial 5-second connection handshake window.

title: Battery State Transition — 'Connecting' to 'Connected' Desync Detection
steps: Launch Poly Studio Mobile app.
Initiate connection to Poly Voyager Free 60+ UC.
During 'Connecting...' state, observe battery area on Home screen (should show dashes or placeholder).
Wait for state to transition to 'Connected'.
Within 1 second of 'Connected' appearing, read battery % for Left, Right, and Charging Case.
Wait 10 seconds. Read battery % again.
expected: Expected: Battery values populate within 1-2 seconds of 'Connected' status. Actual (If Bug): 'Connected' status appears but battery values remain at 0% or show dashes indefinitely, indicating desync between connection state machine and battery data retrieval. Regression boundary: Battery data must populate within 5 seconds of 'Connected' state entry.

title: App Background/Foreground Cycle — Battery Data Retention After Connection
steps: Launch Poly Studio Mobile app. Connect to Poly Voyager Free 60+ UC.
Confirm 'Connected' status and verify battery % shows non-zero values for all three (Left, Right, Case).
Press Home button to background the app.
Wait 15 seconds.
Reopen app from recent apps.
Observe battery % values on Home screen.
Force-kill app (swipe from recents).
Relaunch app. Wait for auto-reconnect. Observe battery % values.
expected: Expected: After background/foreground cycle, battery % values remain populated and correct. After force-kill and relaunch, battery % values re-populate within 5 seconds of auto-reconnect. Actual (If Bug): After background/foreground, battery shows 0% despite 'Connected' status persisting. After kill/relaunch, battery stays at 0% even though device shows 'Connected'. Regression boundary: Battery data must survive app lifecycle transitions without requiring manual disconnect/reconnect.

title: Charging Case Battery — Dedicated Validation for Poly Voyager Free 60+ UC Touchscreen Case
steps: Launch Poly Studio Mobile app. Connect to Poly Voyager Free 60+ UC.
On Home screen, locate the Charging Case battery indicator (separate from earbud indicators).
Record the displayed battery percentage for the case.
Place earbuds in case and close lid. Wait 10 seconds.
Open lid. Re-check Charging Case battery % on Home screen.
Remove earbuds from case. Check Charging Case battery % again.
expected: Expected: Charging Case battery percentage is displayed as a distinct value separate from Left/Right earbud percentages. Value updates appropriately when case is used for charging. Actual (If Bug): Charging Case battery shows 0% or is missing entirely, or incorrectly mirrors one of the earbud battery levels. Regression boundary: The Touchscreen Charge Case battery must be independently read and displayed — it must not default to 0% or inherit earbud battery values.

title: Battery Data Refresh After Bluetooth Disconnect/Reconnect Cycle
steps: Launch Poly Studio Mobile app. Connect to Poly Voyager Free 60+ UC.
Verify all three battery % values are populated (non-zero).
Disconnect Bluetooth from phone settings (not app).
Wait 10 seconds.
Reconnect Bluetooth from phone settings.
Wait for app to show 'Connected' status.
Immediately read battery % for Left, Right, and Charging Case.
Wait 15 seconds. Read battery % again.
expected: Expected: After Bluetooth disconnect/reconnect, battery % values re-populate within 5 seconds of reconnection. Actual (If Bug): After reconnection, battery values remain at 0% despite 'Connected' status, indicating battery data is not re-fetched on reconnection. Regression boundary: Battery data must be re-fetched on every Bluetooth reconnection, not only on initial pairing.

title: Battery Percentage Boundary Values — 0%, 50%, 100% Display Accuracy
steps: Launch Poly Studio Mobile app. Connect to Poly Voyager Free 60+ UC.
Drain or simulate Left Earbud battery to 0% (or near-depletion).
Observe Left Earbud battery % on Home screen.
Charge Left Earbud to approximately 50%. Observe battery %.
Charge Left Earbud to 100%. Observe battery %.
Repeat steps for Right Earbud and Charging Case.
expected: Expected: Battery % accurately reflects true charge level at all boundary values (0%, ~50%, 100%). At 0%, device may show 'Low Battery' warning but percentage must display 0%, not remain stuck at a higher value. Actual (If Bug): Battery shows 0% when device is actually charged, or shows a non-zero value when truly depleted. Regression boundary: The percentage must be a live reading, not a cached/stale value from a previous session.
